# Phase 6.0 — Analyzer + assertion corpus

A fixture set of hand-crafted `ElementSnapshot` + expected analyzer
output pairs, used by `tests/corpus.rs` to regression-test the Rust
implementations of the five Phase 6 analyzers and the 10 assertion DSL
variants.

## Why hand-crafted?

The plan's Phase 6.0 calls for ~50 runner-captured frames + ground-truth
generated by running the Python `qontinui.vision.environment.analyzers`
and `qontinui.vision.verification` modules. That's the eventual target;
this directory is the **scaffolding**:

1. The shape is in place (`frames/<id>.json` + `golden/<id>.json`).
2. A few representative fixtures live here as a smoke set so the Rust
   tests have something to bite on today.
3. `scripts/regenerate_vision_corpus.py` is the one-shot harness that
   walks the runner's `discover` output, runs the Python analyzers, and
   emits matching `golden/<id>.json` files. Future contributors with the
   Python `qontinui` env installed can re-run it to expand the corpus
   to the 50-frame target.

## Directory shape

```
analyzer_corpus/
├── README.md                    (this file)
├── frames/                      ElementSnapshot inputs, one .json per fixture
│   ├── 001-overlap.json
│   ├── 002-alignment-jitter.json
│   ├── 003-tiny-target.json
│   ├── 004-no-clipping-pass.json
│   ├── 005-clipping-fail.json
│   └── …
└── golden/                      Expected analyzer outputs, paired by stem
    ├── 001-overlap.json
    ├── 002-alignment-jitter.json
    └── …
```

Each `golden/<id>.json` is shaped like:

```json
{
  "analyzers": {
    "layout":    { "kinds": ["overlap"], "count_min": 1, "count_max": 4 },
    "elements":  { "kinds": ["tiny_target"], "count_min": 0, "count_max": 10 },
    "color":     { "kinds": [], "count_min": 0, "count_max": 0 },
    "typography":{ "kinds": [], "count_min": 0, "count_max": 0 }
  },
  "assertions": [
    { "assertion": { "type": "no_overlap", "elements": ["btn-a", "btn-b"] },
      "expect_passed": false }
  ]
}
```

`count_min`/`count_max` give the Rust test a tolerance — analyzers are
allowed to surface extra `info` findings without breaking the gate, but
they must hit at least one of the expected `kinds`. Assertion goldens
are exact: pass/fail must match.

## Adding a fixture

1. Capture a small `ElementSnapshot` by hand (or via runner `discover`)
   and save it as `frames/NNN-short-name.json`.
2. Decide what the analyzers + assertions **should** say. Write the
   matching `golden/NNN-short-name.json`.
3. The `corpus` integration test picks it up automatically — no test-
   side wiring needed.

## Regenerating from Python ground truth

When the qontinui Python env is set up:

```bash
# From qontinui repo root:
python scripts/regenerate_vision_corpus.py \
    --frames-dir ../qontinui-schemas/rust-vision-core/tests/fixtures/analyzer_corpus/frames \
    --golden-dir ../qontinui-schemas/rust-vision-core/tests/fixtures/analyzer_corpus/golden \
    --runner-discover-url http://localhost:9876
```

The script reads every `frames/*.json`, calls into
`qontinui.vision.environment.analyzers.*` and
`qontinui.vision.verification.expect.evaluate`, and writes the matching
golden output. See the script header for the full env requirements.

## Acknowledged scope gap

Today this directory ships 5 fixtures (one per analyzer's hot-path
finding). The 50-frame target is a follow-up. The Rust tests still run
the full surface area against what's here — they just don't get the
"95% parity vs Python" gate the plan calls out as the Phase 6.1 merge
criterion. That parity check ships when the corpus grows; the
infrastructure is in place to make that a single commit.
